caveats: inputs.ai_replaceable_share: The supplied 4-digit 5418 wage mix includes sibling advertising industries, so the role allocation is an agency-specific adjustment rather than a direct 541810 observation.
inputs.pi_dist: ANA's compensation evidence is marketer-survey based and does not give an exact small-agency revenue-weighted mix.
inputs.pi_moat: In-house penetration is advertiser-member evidence and may not map to SMB clients of lower-middle-market agencies.
inputs.t50_years: BTOS does not publish a direct 541810 effective-adoption series.
inputs.current_adoption_pct: The observed statistic is national, not an exact advertising-agency rate.
inputs.historical_analogs: The analogy does not prove the pace or magnitude of generative-AI substitution.
inputs.owners_60plus_pct: No NAICS- and EBITDA-band-specific owner-age source was located; confirm seller and management succession case by case.
inputs.owners_60plus_pct.succession_shortage_documented: This deliberately withholds the succession uplift.
inputs.active_consolidators: JEGI's source covers digital marketing services broadly and not a complete count of 541810 buyers.
inputs.buy_mult: Neither source is a direct U.S. 541810 $1–10M EBITDA transaction series; buy multiple must be validated with live brokered comps and QoE.
inputs.exit_mult: Exit multiple depends on net revenue, client retention, data/technology differentiation and normalized owner compensation; it is not an observed agency median.
inputs.pricing_structure: Compensation structures vary by client size and agency type; media billings must be separated from net revenue.

q.rationale: The existing v3 distribution-retention × moat-retention judgment is reused as a bounded Stage 1 commercial-retention proxy.

leaves.rationale: Predeclared bounded demand/operator row for the inherited v3 terminal class; unknown classes remain missing.

e.rationale: Global eligible-union share; heterogeneous records use the predeclared wider/lower class-count row.

n.detail: margin 0.1565 (margins.json[54]: Damodaran Jan 2026: Business & Consumer Services EBITDA/Sales 15.65%); revenue/employee $303,923 (SUSB 2022 receipts/employment at 541810); class <5: 9,620 firms, avg revenue/firm $521,704 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $81,647 -> out of $1-10M band; class 5-9: 1,690 firms, avg revenue/firm $1,849,936 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $289,515 -> out of $1-10M band; class 10-19: 1,101 firms, avg revenue/firm $3,849,742 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $602,485 -> out of $1-10M band; class 20-99: 977 firms, avg revenue/firm $12,049,911 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $1,885,811 -> IN $1-10M band; class 100-499: 198 firms, avg revenue/firm $48,263,556 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $7,553,246 -> IN $1-10M band; class 500+: 96 firms, avg revenue/firm $267,062,250 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $41,795,242 -> out of $1-10M band; n_band = 1,175

s5.rationale: Fixed transform of the disclosed v3 owner-age range into five-year qualifying control-transfer propensity.

a.rationale: Generative production, research, reporting and coordination are materially exposed, while client leadership, brand accountability and sales remain partially human.

l.detail: BLS Industry Productivity (IPS) direct: labor compensation (L02) $33,005.0M / sectoral output (T30) $68,557.8M, year 2022

rho.rationale: Fixed outcome-blind transform of the v3 t50 adoption estimate, widened by the declared bridge error.

inputs.pi_dist: ANA's compensation evidence is marketer-survey based and does not give an exact small-agency revenue-weighted mix.
inputs.pi_moat: In-house penetration is advertiser-member evidence and may not map to SMB clients of lower-middle-market agencies.
inputs.t50_years: BTOS does not publish a direct 541810 effective-adoption series.
inputs.current_adoption_pct: The observed statistic is national, not an exact advertising-agency rate.
inputs.historical_analogs: The analogy does not prove the pace or magnitude of generative-AI substitution.
inputs.owners_60plus_pct: No NAICS- and EBITDA-band-specific owner-age source was located; confirm seller and management succession case by case.

source_review.summary: Mechanics, identity, dataset injection, fresh finalization, memo rendering, digests and arithmetic all pass. The sources support the directional task-exposure, compensation, in-housing and M&A claims, and the PRESSURED terminal view remains distinct from current capture economics. Publication requires all authored caveats because the role mix, adoption bridge, client survey scope, owner age, buyer count and transaction multiples are not direct 541810 lower-middle-market observations.

sources.title: NAICS 541810 Advertising Agencies definition
sources.what_it_supports: The exact 541810 scope, included services and exclusions for media buying, design, marketing consulting and media representatives.

sources.title: The Microstructure of AI Diffusion: Evidence From Firms, Business Functions, and Worker Tasks
sources.what_it_supports: National BTOS AI-use levels, higher knowledge-intensive sector adoption and limited functional breadth among adopting firms.

sources.title: ANA: Labor-Based Fees Remain Dominant Form Of Agency Compensation
sources.what_it_supports: Agency-compensation structure, including predominance of labor-based fees and small-advertiser use of labor-based agreements.

sources.title: ANA In-House Agencies research summary
sources.what_it_supports: In-house agency penetration and reported cost/time efficiency evidence; this is advertiser-member evidence, not a small-agency revenue survey.

sources.title: JEGI CLARITY notes increased M&A activity within digital marketing services
sources.what_it_supports: Directional marketing-services M&A activity and the reported 10.8x median for £4m–£7.5m EBITDA digital-marketing assets.

sources.title: Marketing Agency EBITDA Multiples & Valuations — 2025
sources.what_it_supports: A secondary agency-valuation reference describing 8–12x top-end sales for high-growth, low-concentration agencies; used only as a non-comparable ceiling reference.

title: Advertising Agencies
